title: Stash reconciliation (v25 cycle 14 entry; per user rule: never DROP)
author: claude opus 4.7
rule: User rule: 'never drop a stash always reconcile'. Both stashes preserved in git stash list indefinitely. This worklog documents their classification and reconciliation actions.

content: DELETES .cargo/config.toml (v14 T2 thin LTO + sccache hints)
stashes.supersession_evidence: Both files exist on HEAD @ 18a5adfb67: ls -la .cargo/config.toml .github/workflows/cache-stats-pages.yml both return file metadata. The v14-t2 thin LTO + sccache hints were landed via commit e6ef56f01c (v14 T2 on apps-extract-all) which preserves + extends the files rather than deleting them.
stashes.reconciliation: WORKLOG entry only. No action on the stash. Stash stays in git stash list per user rule.

stashes.unique_evidence: HEAD @ 18a5adfb67 pheno-config/Cargo.toml [dev-dependencies] contains ONLY proptest = "1.4" (with criterion scaffolded but commented). No signal-hook, tempfile, or tokio reference. The stash introduces a signal-driven hot-reload dev-dependency stack for L33 (config hot-reload per v21 T5 plan).
stashes.reconciliation: WORKLOG entry + future cherry-pick to a working branch (e.g., feat/v26-l33-hot-reload-2026-06-25). Cherry-pick NOT pop (pop would clear the stash, violating user rule). Stash stays in git stash list per user rule.
stashes.user_question: Open cherry-pick PR feat/v26-l33-hot-reload-2026-06-25 from stash@{1}? Or defer to v26 cycle 15?
